q: Every reaction has a rate, or _______, at which it proceeds.
Explosion of dynamite, striking of a match, and burning of magnesium ribbon take within a _______.
_______ of rocks and rusting of iron pipe happen for years.
_______ of ozone layer depends on the relative rates of reactions that produce and destroy ozone molecules.
_______ reaction is essential on food preservatives.
The economical synthesis of ammonia to produce _______ depends on the rate at which gaseous nitrogen and hydrogen can be converted to ammonia.
The study of reaction rates is known as "_______".
_______ is a measure of how fast or how slow the reaction is.
An iron nail reacts slowly in the air as it _______.
White phosphorus bursts into flames when exposed to _______.
The reactions with the oxygen in the air take place at _______ rates.
Rate of a reaction is measured in terms of the change in amount of a _______ or product per unit of time.
The rate of a reaction is measured in terms of a decrease in the concentration of the reactant or an increase in the concentration of product per unit time at constant _______.
Reactant particles moving in random directions possess _______ energy.
When the particles frequently _______ with one another, the energy is transferred as a result of the collision.
Not all _______ between reactant particles result in the formation of reaction products.
For a reaction to occur colliding particles must impact with certain _______ known as an activation energy.
For a reaction to take place, reactant particles or molecules must be moving and _______ with each other.
During a collision, the spatial _______ of the reactant molecules is important to determine whether or not a reaction occurs.
If the colliding particles have the incorrect orientation, they will _______ each other.
The unsuccessful orientations with the _______ energy do not produce a reaction.
When the particles collide with the _______ orientation, they must have enough energy for a successful collision to produce a reaction.
The rate of reaction depends on the rate of _______ collisions between reactant particles.
Reacting particles must collide with sufficient energy to form the _______.
Activated complex is a temporary _______ arrangement of atoms that may form products or break apart to reform the reactants.
The more successful _______ there are, the more the activated complex lead to the rate of reaction being faster.
Chemical reactions depend on the collisions between the reacting particles – atoms, _______ or ions.
_______ theory states that a chemical reaction only takes place when two reacting molecules collide in the correct orientation with sufficient kinetic energy.
According to collision theory, a reaction will _______ if the frequency of collision increases.
For a chemical reaction to occur, the colliding molecules must possess a certain minimum _______ necessary to rearrange the original bonds breaking or new bonds forming.
_______ energy is the minimum amount of energy that reacting particles must have to form the activated complex and lead to a reaction.
During the progress of a reaction, an _______ intermediate is known as an activated complex.
An activated complex having minimum energy is formed before producing a _______.
In order to get the product, the reactants must pass through a short-lived, _______ transition state.
Transition state is also known as an _______ state.
Transition state is a highly _______ state that would be highly unstable.
The minimum energy that needs to be reached in the _______ state is equal to the activation energy (Ea) for that reaction.
The activation energy represents an _______ for the reaction.
When there is sufficient energy to overcome the energy barrier, the formation of the _______ is favourable.
If a reaction has low Ea, the reaction rate is _______.
Increasing the concentrations of the reactants _______ the rate of reactions.
In reactions involving solids, a reaction occurs at the _______ surface between reactants.
The rate of reaction involving a _______ reactant is increased by increasing its surface area.
The _______ the particle size, the large the surface area will be.
If the surface area of the reactants is larger, the rate of reaction will be _______.
Aluminium foil moderately react with sodium hydroxide solution when _______.
Powdered aluminium reacts _______ with sodium hydroxide solution in the cold.
Large pieces of wood are difficult to ignite whereas small pieces burn more _______.
Changing the _______ does not affect the rate of reaction that only involves solids or liquids.
Pressure affects the rate of reaction involving _______.
The pressure is inversely proportional to the _______ of a gas at constant temperature.
At _______ pressure, the volume becomes larger and the number of gas molecules is smaller, and the rate of reaction is slower.
At _______ pressure, the volume becomes smaller, the number of collisions is greater, and the rate of reaction is faster.
Food can cook _______ in a pressure cooker than in a pot.
Most chemical reactions react _______ at a high temperature than at a low temperature.
The kinetic energy of a gas is directly proportional to the _______.
At high temperature, the molecules move faster and collide more frequently with sufficient _______ energy for the reaction to proceed.
The number of effective collisions per second of the reacting particles _______, and the rate of reaction increases.
Foods are cooled or frozen to _______ the chemical reaction that result in the spoiling of food, the ripening of fruit and the souring of milk.
A catalyst is a substance that _______ the rate of a chemical reaction.
A _______ remains chemically unchanged at the end of the reaction.
_______ catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction.
Iron is used as a catalyst in the Haber Process for manufacturing of _______.
Vanadium (V) oxide or platinized asbestos are used as a catalyst in the Contact Process for the manufacturing of _______.
_______ can be used as a catalyst in the preparation of oxygen from hydrogen peroxide.
Sometimes, a chemical reaction needs to go more slowly or _______ unwanted reaction.
The _______ acts as a catalyst to slow down the oxidation of sodium sulphate to sodium sulphite.
Negative catalyst _______ the rate of a chemical reaction.
The rate of catalysed reaction is _______ than that of uncatalyzed reaction.
_______ is a portion of a physical state that is homogeneous throughout.
Homogeneous catalyst is a catalyst which is in the _______ phase as the reaction in a catalysed reaction.
_______ catalysts most often catalyse the gaseous and aqueous reactions to increase their reaction rates.
_______ catalyst is a catalyst which is in a different phase from the reactions in a catalysed reaction.
Most heterogeneous catalysts are _______ that catalyse either gaseous or liquid reactants.
Enzymes are _______ that act as catalysts for specific biochemical reactions.
Ptyalin is found in _______ and pepsin in gastric juice.
The reaction rate using _______ is much faster than inorganic catalysts, often increased by a factor of 10^6 to 10^12.
Some chemical reactions obtain the _______ they need from light.
Photochemical reaction is a chemical reaction initiated by the _______ of energy.
The _______ the light, the faster the reaction.
If the _______ of light is greater, the more reactant molecules gain the required energy and faster the reaction speed.
When reactant molecules absorb the light (hν), a _______ reaction takes place.
The reaction between hydrogen and chlorine is extremely slow in the _______.
The reaction between hydrogen and chlorine becomes _______ when the reaction mixture is exposed to intense sunlight.
The large crystals of sugar burn _______ than finely ground sugar.
Foods readily spoil at room temperature at a _______ rate than when foods are refrigerated.
The unit for the rate of a chemical reaction in solution is _______.
The _______ energy required to form the activated complex is called the activation energy.
A catalyst which is in the different phases of the reactants is known as a _______.
The correct _______ of reactant molecules leads to the formation of products.
More molecules have sufficient energy to react, so the _______ of reaction is increased.
